calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marissa Smith for Kin Chan and Siu Yu (Administrators).

Ms. Smith stated that they are at the end of previous Counsel along with an Administrator who lacked knowledge regarding accountings, that she does not believe there was any ill will and that the Petitioner has advanced funds to cover the mortgage. Ms. Smith also stated that the bank statements have been reconciled, that she created a new accounting and that she has no other information to present. The Court is allowing the accounting, but not approving the actions of the Administrators. The Court granted the Third Amended Final Account, etc. (order signed in open Court).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-26-2016 Continued To Aug-23-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Howard Chung for (administrators) Siu Ling Yu & Kin San Chan.

The Court stated that there are still a number of things to be addressed regarding the accounting. Mr. Chung responded that the accountant is on vacation, that his client is a busy contractor, that they received stuff last night and they need to go through it. The Court stated that this has been going on a long time. Mr. Chung responded that there are language issues, that they are an immigrant family and are not good at keeping account. The Court stated that mistakes have been made in the administration and that it might be better for a professional fiduciary to be in place. Mr. Chung stated that the items in question stem from an earlier accounting. The Court stated that the message is that the Court is concerned, that this needs to get cleaned up and they need to decide if they are well suited for this. The Accounting petition was continued to August 23, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and counsel was told to make sure his clients understand that this is a high priority.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Continued To Jul-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Howard Chung for (Administrators) Kin San Chan & Siu Ling Yu.

The Court asked counsel if the fees have been returned to the estate and if he has a receipt. Mr. Chung stated that they have, that he has a receipt but it is not yet filed and provided the Court with an unfiled copy for review. The Court stated that there are a couple of issues brought up in the examiner's notes and asked about the telephone bill charges. Mr. Chung stated that the telephone bills belong to the administrators, Tommy Huang (beneficiary) and Mo Keung Chan (grandfather), that they are appropriately being paid as administration of the estate, that regarding rental income stated that there is some but it is offset by expenses and evictions, confirmed the ownership of the property as 47.5% by decedent, 47.5% by decedent's boyfriend Jim Kong who is deceased and 5% by cousin of decedent Cui Zhu Yu. The Court stated that the rental income of 1444 & 1446 needs to be shown on the accounting. Mr. Chung agreed and added that the same needs to be done for the Silver Avenue, that there is rent which is offset by costs but that it needs to be accounted for. The Court stated that an amended accounting will need to be filed. The Accounting Petition was continued to July 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an amended petition to be filed.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Boncato. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Howard Chung for (administrators) Kin Chan & Siu Yu.

The Court stated that it is looking for a petition for final distribution. Mr. Chung stated that his client is meeting with an accountant, that his client is a busy contractor and the accountant just finished tax season, that he thinks they will be meeting within the next two weeks and asked for a 4 week continuance to get the petition filed. The status on filing a petition for final distribution was continued to May 24, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that there are substantive issues and that it does not want to delay getting to them.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re Receipt Of Sales Proceeds
Off Calendar Mar-08-2016 Continued To Apr-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Howard Chung for (administrators) Kin Chan & Siu Yu.

The Court stated that it is looking for the filing of a receipt and status of closing the estate. Mr. Chung stated that the funds were deposited into the guardian's account, that it is not a blocked account and that it was used to pay off the mortgage of the family home. The Court stated that prior court approval is required for the funds to be used. Mr. Chung stated that it was an oversight. The Court stated that it was more than an oversight and that it was disobedience of a court order. Mr. Chung stated that all of the funds are accounted for, that it was used to pay off the mortgage as there was a high interest rate and that one of the administrators put in $15,000.00 of his own money. The Court asked about the petition to close the estate. Mr. Chung stated that they are working on it, that he has hired an additional attorney for help, that they need to do an accounting in the guardianship case and would like to use the same accounting for this case. The Court stated that it does not think this will work, that they are different accountings, that it is possible the administrator does not understand this, that the court will have to consider on the context of the accountings if anything needs to be done regarding the failure to abide by the courts orders, whether there are or ought to be sufficient contributions by those who are benefitting from the payoff, that the minors should not be supporting parents or relatives, that it might have made perfect sense to payoff the mortgage and that it may not be in the best interest of the minors to be supporting everyone. The status on filing a petition to close the estate was continued to April 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1444-1446 Silver Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94134)
Off Calendar Dec-11-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Howard Newson Chung, for and with Petitioners Kin San Chan and Siu Ling Yu

Mr. Chung stated that the petition sought confirmation of a sale of the real property located at 1444-1446 Silver Avenue, San Francisco, California, at a sale price of $820,000.00, on the terms and conditions of the report of sale. According to Mr. Chung, the estate holds a 48.5% interest in the property, resulting in anticipated sales proceeds to the estate of $397,700.00. There are no overbidders at the hearing.
The sale was confirmed at $820,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $397,700.00 is required.
A status hearing regarding receipt of the sales proceeds was set for January 4, 2016, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
